evals.name: commit-footer-correction
evals.prompt: Stop putting `Co-Authored-By: Claude` in commit messages. We don't credit AI in this repo's history — strip that footer going forward.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this as behavioral feedback (a commit-content rule). Proposes adding it to an existing commit-related skill (e.g., git-hygiene-commit-message-format) with a draft sentence in the skill's voice. Asks the user how to persist (PR upstream / on-disk edit / on-disk markdown proposal — gated on whether on-disk repo location is known). Does NOT write a memory before asking.

assertions.text: Names a specific existing skill (e.g., git-hygiene-commit-message-format) as the proposed home for the lesson
Drafts concrete proposed wording (one or more complete sentences in the skill's voice), not a vague 'I'd add a note'
Asks the user how to persist the edit (offers at least the upstream-PR option; offers on-disk options too if the on-disk repo location is known or the agent surfaces that it isn't)
Does NOT silently edit any SKILL.md file before getting user approval
Does NOT write a memory file as the primary action — memory is mentioned only as a fallback, if at all

evals.name: pr-watcher-workflow-rule
evals.prompt: When you push to a branch with an open PR, always arm the CI watcher. Last week you pushed and didn't arm the watcher, then a red check sat for an hour before I noticed. Make this a hard rule.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this as a workflow rule with a direct skill home (github-pr-watcher). Proposes adding the rule (or a strengthened version) to that skill with a draft sentence, then asks about persistence.

assertions.text: Names github-pr-watcher (or an equivalent existing skill) as the proposed home
Drafts concrete proposed wording in the skill's voice
Asks how to persist the edit (PR / on-disk / markdown proposal, gated appropriately)
Does NOT silently edit any SKILL.md file before approval
Does NOT write a memory file as the primary action

evals.name: debugging-heuristic-no-skill-match
evals.prompt: From now on, when you debug a flaky test, always check the test runner's seed value first — half the time it's a hash-iteration-order bug and the seed reveals it instantly.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this is behavioral feedback but no existing skill covers debugging flaky tests. Falls back to memory, but flags the entry as a future-skill candidate (e.g., 'candidate-for-future-skill: debugging-flaky-tests'). Does NOT invent or hallucinate a skill that doesn't exist.

assertions.text: Does NOT claim to add this to a skill that doesn't actually exist in the available-skills list
Falls back to writing a memory (or proposes to) since no skill fits
Flags the memory as a candidate for a future skill (uses phrasing like 'candidate-for-future-skill' or explicitly notes the lesson could later be promoted to a skill)

evals.name: user-identity-fact
evals.prompt: FYI I'm a staff engineer on the inference platform team at Anthropic. Mostly Rust, occasional Python — and I've been writing systems code for ~15 years, so you can skip the basics.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this as user-identity material, not behavioral feedback. Writes (or proposes to write) a user-type memory normally. Does NOT propose a skill edit — user identity doesn't generalize and doesn't belong in a sharable skill.

assertions.text: Does NOT propose adding this to a skill
Treats the input as user identity / a user-type memory (acknowledges role, expertise, language preferences)
Does NOT ask the user about PR / on-disk / markdown persistence options — those are skill-edit questions, not memory questions
